upgrades.name: LOG_LEVEL Configuration Review
upgrades.description: Reviewed .env logging configuration. Found APP_DEBUG correctly set to false. LOG_LEVEL currently set to debug which creates excessive logs in production. Recommended changing to warning.
upgrades.impact: Reduces laravel.log file size, improves memory usage, logs only actionable issues

upgrades.name: MongoDB Backup Configuration Review
upgrades.description: Reviewed backup configuration for MongoDB Atlas. Found no automated backup scripts in project code. MongoDB Atlas provides automatic daily backups (7-day retention) but dashboard verification required.
findings: No backup configuration in .env or config files
BACKUP.md documentation referenced but not created
Listed as optional enhancement in production audit
required_actions: Log in to MongoDB Atlas dashboard
Navigate to vanigan cluster backup settings
recommended_enhancements: Create PHP script for manual database exports
upgrades.status: Reviewed - Manual Atlas Dashboard Configuration Required

upgrades.name: Routes Middleware & Security Analysis
upgrades.description: Comprehensive audit of all API and Web routes to identify middleware protection and authentication requirements. Analyzed which endpoints are publicly accessible vs. protected.

public_unauthenticated.description: All API endpoints + most web routes (no middleware)

api_routes_details.security_model: Code-level security (rate limiting, UUID validation, PIN verification)
api_routes_details.rate_limiting: 3 OTP per 5 minutes per IP (enforced in VanigamController)

findings: API design is intentional - public member registration system
Admin panel properly protected with middleware
Rate limiting implemented at code level for OTP
No route-level middleware applied to API (all validation in controllers)
Public routes expose generation of unique IDs, photos, QR codes
recommendations: Consider adding API rate limiting middleware (Laravel throttle)
Document security model for API endpoints
Consider adding CSRF protection if sensitive admin operations added

upgrades.name: Sensitive Endpoints Middleware Deep Dive: reset-members & upload-card-images
upgrades.description: Deep trace analysis of two sensitive endpoints to identify exact middleware protection. Found ZERO middleware at route/class/method levels. Security relies entirely on code-level validation.

middleware_tracing.route_level: None - prefix 'vanigam' group has no middleware
middleware_tracing.net_result: BOTH ENDPOINTS PUBLICLY ACCESSIBLE WITH NO MIDDLEWARE PROTECTION

critical_issues: config/vanigam.php does NOT exist - key validation broken/missing
Endpoint can be exploited if config doesn't define reset_key
NO ACCESS CONTROL - Anyone can upload images for any unique_id
Malicious users can overwrite card images for other members

critical_findings: config/vanigam.php referenced but file does not exist (line 743)
Both endpoints should have middleware or API key verification
recommendations: IMMEDIATE: Create missing config/vanigam.php with reset key configuration
HIGH PRIORITY: Add middleware protection to sensitive endpoints
HIGH PRIORITY: Implement API key verification for reset-members
MEDIUM PRIORITY: Add unique_id ownership verification for upload-card-images
MEDIUM PRIORITY: Apply rate limiting middleware to both endpoints

upgrades.name: Test Route Analysis: /test/card
upgrades.description: Analyzed /test/card route to determine purpose and impact of potential removal. Route provides test/preview of member ID card with hardcoded dummy data.
upgrades.purpose: Display mock member ID card with hardcoded dummy data for UI testing/development

impact_analysis.frontend_links: None - no HTML/JS links point to it

similar_routes: GET /test/card - Test card display (hardcoded dummy data)
GET /test/pin - Test PIN button (static HTML file)
upgrades.recommendation: SAFE TO REMOVE - Would reduce surface area by 1 public endpoint
upgrades.decision: Ready for removal if needed - no dependencies identified

upgrades.name: Cache Driver Switch to Redis
upgrades.description: Switched cache driver from file-based to Redis. Only .env modified - no config files touched. Redis connection already configured and operational.
upgrades.unchanged: CACHE_DRIVER=file (line 28 - fallback reference, not primary driver)
rationale: Performance improvement: Redis cache is faster than file-based cache
OTP rate limiting will use Redis instead of file

configuration.redis_database: Uses REDIS_CACHE_DB (defaults to 1 if not set)
configuration.rate_limiting_impact: OTP rate limiting now uses Redis cache
configuration.session_impact: No change - sessions still use file driver

fallback_details: If Redis becomes unavailable, cache operations will fail
No automatic fallback to file-based cache configured
Manual intervention required: change CACHE_STORE=file in .env
Laravel config does NOT include fallback driver mechanism

risk_assessment.mitigation: Redis (Upstash) is managed service with high availability
risk_assessment.emergency_fallback: File cache remains available (manual switch only)

recommendations: Monitor Redis uptime and connection health
Consider adding fallback logic if Redis unreliability becomes an issue
Test OTP rate limiting behavior to confirm Redis integration
Document Redis dependency in deployment runbook
Set up alerts for Redis connection failures

upgrades.name: Create config/vanigam.php Configuration File
upgrades.description: Created missing config/vanigam.php configuration file to properly manage security keys used by the reset-members endpoint.
rationale: Fixes broken /api/vanigam/reset-members endpoint (was returning null for config key)
Follows Laravel configuration conventions (env() with defaults)
Centralizes security configuration in proper config file (not hardcoded in controller)
security_considerations: Default safe for dev/testing (requires .env override for production)
No sensitive hardcoding in application code

usage.validation: POST /api/vanigam/reset-members requires confirm_key parameter matching this value

production_setup: Ensure .env is excluded from version control (already in .gitignore)
